verses.text: The people pray that the LORD will answer their king in trouble, send him help from the sanctuary, and remember his offerings. They surround him with intercession. It is a blessed thing when a people uphold their leader before God.

The people pray that the LORD will answer their king in trouble, send him help from the sanctuary, and remember his offerings. They surround him with intercession. It is a blessed thing when a people uphold their leader before God.

The people pray that the LORD will answer their king in trouble, send him help from the sanctuary, and remember his offerings. They surround him with intercession. It is a blessed thing when a people uphold their leader before God.

They ask God to grant his heart's desire and fulfil his plans, that they may rejoice in his victory. Their hopes are bound to his. The cause of God's anointed is the joy and concern of all His people.

They ask God to grant his heart's desire and fulfil his plans, that they may rejoice in his victory. Their hopes are bound to his. The cause of God's anointed is the joy and concern of all His people.

'Now know I that the LORD saveth his anointed'; some trust in chariots and horses, but 'we will remember the name of the LORD.' Confidence rests not in arms but in God. The world leans on its strength; faith leans on the Lord and stands.

'Now know I that the LORD saveth his anointed'; some trust in chariots and horses, but 'we will remember the name of the LORD.' Confidence rests not in arms but in God. The world leans on its strength; faith leans on the Lord and stands.

'Now know I that the LORD saveth his anointed'; some trust in chariots and horses, but 'we will remember the name of the LORD.' Confidence rests not in arms but in God. The world leans on its strength; faith leans on the Lord and stands.
'Save, LORD: let the king hear us when we call.' The psalm ends in a brief, urgent plea. All their trust gathers into one cry for God to save.
